text: Here's what science actually says about dendritic cells. Dendritic cells are like spies for your immune system, patrolling for invaders. When they detect pathogens, they capture and process them. Research shows they activate T-cells, triggering an immune response. But did you know some studies suggest they might also play a role in cancer treatment? Understanding these cells could revolutionize medicine, especially in immunotherapy. Want to dive deeper into the world of cells? Hit that follow for more!
